# Distribution Channels and Role Model

<!-- Generated by go run ./tools/distributionhostinvariantsdocs -write-doc. Do not edit by hand. -->

[Back to distribution host invariants](../invariants.md)

## Distribution Channels

| Channel | Meaning | Primary purpose |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `developer-id` | macOS Developer ID signed and notarized outside the Mac App Store | Primary macOS distribution |
| `mac-app-store` | Mac App Store sandboxed app | Long-term restricted Store channel |
| `msix-sideload` | Signed MSIX outside Microsoft Store | Primary Windows distribution |
| `msix-store` | Microsoft Store MSIX / packaged desktop app | Windows Store distribution |
| `dev-local` | Repository, launchd, or foreground development | Developer-local execution |

A distribution channel is package artifact identity. The same daemon binary may expose different host surfaces when the channel changes.

## Role Model

| Role | Owns | Must not own |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Store App | Onboarding, provider path registration, workspace selection, background/privacy settings, status and review UI | Provider CLI binaries, silent installers, unmanaged updaters |
| Local Helper / Broker | Local-only IPC, task claim loop, provider runtime orchestration, status and metrics | External TCP listeners, autostart without consent |
| Provider Connector | Executable provenance, version/login/capability probe | Vendor code redistribution, provider license substitution |
| SaaS Control Plane | Assignment, polling, event stream, policy-aware routing metadata | Customer PC provider process execution |

- GUI adapters convert provider path, login status, workspace grant, and consent state into C11 records.
- GUI adapters do not spawn provider processes; execution stays behind the local helper and C4 runtime boundary.
- Shared wire types move to `riido-contracts` only when two repositories need the same contract.
